skillName: Substituting into expressions with multiple operations
misconceptionSummary: Students think substitution changes the order of the original expression instead of simply turning it into arithmetic.

visuals.subText: The structure of the expression stays the same

steps.narration: Substitution does not change the shape of the expression. It only replaces letters with numbers.
Notice that the multiplication and subtraction stay exactly where they were. Only the variable is replaced.
Because substitution gives an arithmetic expression, the usual order of operations now matters.

lines.text: More operations means more care after substitution

steps.narration: The challenge in multiple-operation substitution is not the replacement itself. It is evaluating the resulting arithmetic expression accurately.

visuals.expression: Keep the structure, replace the variable, then evaluate
visuals.label: Substitution preserves the original expression

steps.narration: That is the key understanding. The expression keeps its structure, and substitution simply makes it numerical.

misconceptionStrip.text: Common mistake: rearranging the expression after substitution. The operations stay in the same order and places as before.
misconceptionStrip.audioNarration: Do not rebuild the expression from scratch. Keep the original structure and replace only the variable.
